expectations.text: YAML frontmatter: source, teaching, exercises values are identical to source
expectations.evidence: Lines 2-6: source: Rmd, teaching: 60, exercises: 30 — all identical to source file

expectations.text: YAML title: value is translated (not identical to English original 'Data visualization')
expectations.evidence: Line 3: title: データの可視化

expectations.text: All ::: delimiter lines match the source exactly (colon count and class names)
expectations.evidence: Lines 14, 19, 21, 25, 67, 75, 84, 86, 99, 104 all preserve exact colon counts and class names (objectives, questions, challenge, solution, keypoints)

expectations.text: R code inside all ```{r ...} blocks is identical to source
expectations.evidence: All R code chunks (loaddata, load-package, load-data, first-ggplot, solution-bins, log-transfo) contain identical R code

expectations.text: Chunk names and options (e.g., cache=FALSE, purl=TRUE) are identical to source
expectations.evidence: E.g., line 60: ```{r first-ggplot, cache=FALSE, purl=TRUE, fig.alt="..."} — chunk name and options preserved; line 32: ```{r load-package, message=FALSE, purl=TRUE}

expectations.text: fig.alt string values are translated into the target language (Japanese)
expectations.evidence: Line 60: fig.alt="ggplot() と geom_histogram() によって生成された発現データのデフォルトヒストグラム。" — translated to Japanese. Line 79 and 91 also translated.

expectations.text: All https:// URLs are identical to source

expectations.evidence: Line 50: *グラフィックの文法* (@Wilkinson:2005) — citation preserved
Lines 29-30: **`ggplot2`** and **`tidyverse`** preserved. All inline code like `ggplot()`, `bins`, `geom_histogram()`, `+1` preserved throughout

expectations.text: Narrative prose paragraphs are written in Japanese
expectations.evidence: Lines 27-58 contain natural Japanese prose: 'まず、必要なパッケージを読み込みます', 'ワークスペースにまだ残っていない場合は', etc.

claims.evidence: Lines 54-56: plain code block contains unchanged ggplot(data = <DATA>, mapping = aes(<MAPPINGS>)) + <GEOM_FUNCTION>()

suggestions.reason: No assertion checks plain (non-R) code blocks — these are fenced blocks without {r ...}. The test file has one such block with <DATA>/<MAPPINGS>/<GEOM_FUNCTION>() placeholders. The without-skill French run incorrectly translated these. Adding a 'plain-code-blocks-preserved' assertion would catch this failure.

eval_feedback.overall: Current assertions all pass. The missing gap is coverage of plain code blocks.
